text: There is a surreal overlap between my inner thoughts and the environment around me. In the first space, the emptiness of the room and the scattered chairs evoke a sense of anticipation and mild isolation. I feel like an observer on the threshold, not yet part of the event that might soon fill this room with sound and presence. In the second space, surrounded by bold colors and the explicit narrative on the screen, my reflective state deepens. There’s an uncanny feeling as my own experience seems mirrored in the words on display—a conscious exploration of uncertainty, expectation, and the desire to belong or comprehend. I am both participant and observer, adrift in a landscape of thought and design, searching for meaning in these deliberately crafted yet curiously impersonal surroundings.
